nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Negative)
widgets_values: Not used by Flux2-dev but required input by some nodes.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt)
widgets_values:
A photorealistic digital illustration of a beautiful woman with soft, soft rounded facial features anchored by a broad, wide, structured, gently squared jawline, with a softly rounded chin. She has vibrant green eyes, framed by light faint blonde eyebrows, thick lashes, and styled pink lips. Her nose is small, pretty, and slightly upturned. She has  voluminous, long wavy platinum blonde hair with soft curls that is dramatically parted to the side. She has hyper-femenine hourglass figure with firm round breasts. Her skin is glowing, glossy, and sun-kissed.

Plain white background, polished aesthetic. She stands in a dynamic contrapposto pose; her right arm is raised high and bent at the elbow with her hand resting behind her hair, while her left arm is raised to shoulder level with the hand relaxed in a graceful gesture.

She is wearing a sleek, light iridescent strapless bathing suit that exposes her shoulders, with a deep plunging V-neckline that continues as a line of keyhole cutouts down its midline that gives a glimpse of her smooth toned body. The bathing suit has subtle shaping underbust ribbing and subtle silver accents. Her arms are raised gracefully, with one hand resting near her long hair and the other bent elegantly at the wrist. Her skin and outfit exhibit a subtle, premium glossy reflection. She wears classic, thick metallic silver hoop earrings, silver arm and wrist bracelets and her shoulders are slightly turned to accentuate the dynamic, curved posture.

nodes.title: TRUE = SET UP CACHE; FALSE  = RUN WORKFLOW
Important Note!
widgets_values: Run with true to set cache control image cache. Then run again with false to launch workflow. Time and memory pressure of a first run with control net and/or after a server start is cut significantly in a 16 GB VRAM laptop. Rerun with true every time new control images are loaded.

nodes.title: Important Note: Low VRAM Tip
widgets_values:
If you're running on a lower-VRAM GPU (this workflow was validated on a 16 GB laptop GPU), I strongly recommend using the Cache Prep pass before inference.

* Set both cache-prep switches to True and run the workflow once.
This precomputes and stores all Reference Image, ControlNet Image, and Inpaint Context latents in CPU RAM.
* Set both switches back to False and run the workflow normally.

During inference, the cached latents are reused instead of being regenerated, eliminating repeated VAE encoding and reducing unnecessary model loading/unloading. This significantly lowers VRAM pressure, speeds up execution, and makes larger or more complex workflows practical on memory-constrained systems.

Tip: Once the caches are warm, you can experiment with prompts, seeds, and generation settings without rebuilding the cached latents unless you change the reference images, ControlNet inputs, masks, or sampling geometry.

Look for the nodes with yellow faces in this workflow.

groups.title: Set Up Image Cache at First Run Reduces Memory Churn in Low VRAM Systems
